mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the t-statistic of mean return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in greater than 50% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in greater than 55% of observations during early-cycle phases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold, or the inflation premium fails to materialize in equity pricing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the unemployment rate and the federal funds rate signal stagflationary pressures and policy-induced demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic expansion and supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in greater than 60% of quarters during recovery phases, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for prior equity momentum.
